Partitions are the most critical fault type — they test whether the saga system correctly detects unreachable participants and triggers compensation. Implementation: (1) Partition model: define which actor pairs can communicate (connectivity graph), (2) Partition injection: block message delivery between partitioned actors, (3) Partition healing: restore connectivity after configurable duration, (4) Partial partition: asymmetric partitions where A can reach B but not vice versa. Pass criteria: (a) Saga compensation triggers correctly under partition, (b) No split-brain — partitioned actors do not make conflicting decisions, (c) Recovery after partition heal — system converges to consistent state, (d) Budget/deadline expiry during partition causes correct cancellation.  {"id":"bd-2ktrc.3","title":"[TESTING] Implement fault injection for crash/restart","description":"Implement fault injection for simulating actor crash and restart in Asupersync. Crash faults test whether the supervision tree correctly detects failed actors, cleans up their obligations, and restarts them. Implementation: (1) Crash injection: kill actor at random point during execution (after N operations or at specific protocol step), (2) Restart model: restart actor with fresh state (cold restart) or from last checkpoint (warm restart), (3) Cascade crash: crash supervisor to test supervision tree recovery. Lab runtime integration: crashes are scheduled events with deterministic timing.  {"id":"bd-2ktrc.4","title":"[TESTING] Implement fault injection for clock skew","description":"Implement fault injection for simulating clock skew between Asupersync actors. Clock skew tests whether timeout-based protocols (lease expiry, budget deadlines, heartbeat detection) remain correct when clocks diverge.

BACKGROUND: In distributed systems, clock skew is a fundamental source of bugs. Even with NTP, clocks can diverge by milliseconds (normal) to seconds (pathological). The CALM theorem (Graveyard section 13.3) notes that monotone programs are clock-insensitive, but threshold-based operations (lease expiry, timeout detection) are inherently clock-dependent. Testing with simulated clock skew validates that the system uses causal ordering (logical clocks) rather than wall-clock assumptions for correctness-critical decisions.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Define ClockSkewInjector struct with per_actor_offset: HashMap<ActorId, Duration> for static skew and drift_rate: HashMap<ActorId, f64> for progressive drift (ms/s). (2) Hook into the virtual time system from bd-1hu19.3: each actors VirtualClock applies its offset before returning the current time. (3) Clock jump: schedule a sudden offset change at a specific virtual time point to simulate NTP correction. (4) Monotonicity violation: temporarily return a time earlier than the previous read (detects code that assumes monotonic clocks). (5) Integration with FrankenLab scenario YAML: clock_skew section in fault schedule specifying per-actor offsets, drift rates, and jump events. (6) Use quanta crate for high-resolution clock abstraction that can be swapped for virtual clock in tests.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Lease expiry is correct despite clock skew -- use causal ordering, not wall-clock, for lease validity determination. Specifically: a lease acquired at logical time T with duration D expires at logical time T+D regardless of wall-clock skew. (2) Budget deadlines account for worst-case skew -- budget remaining calculation uses the maximum clock reading across all participants. (3) Heartbeat detection uses logical clocks -- a heartbeat received with a different wall-clock timestamp is accepted if the logical clock is within bounds. (4) No liveness violation -- system makes progress despite bounded clock skew (verified: all sagas complete within 2x the non-skewed baseline time). (5) Clock jump does not cause spurious lease expiry or budget exhaustion. (6) Monotonicity violation is detected and logged as a warning (system continues, does not crash).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each clock skew injection emits structured log: actor_id, original_time_ms, skewed_time_ms, offset_ms, drift_rate, event_type (static_offset|drift|jump|monotonicity_violation). All injected faults logged to EvidenceLedger: component=clock_skew_injector, action=skew_applied, top_features includes max_skew_ms, affected_actor_count. Aggregate stats at test completion: max_observed_skew_ms, monotonicity_violations_count, lease_expiry_correctness (pass/fail count).

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Unit tests: verify ClockSkewInjector correctly applies offsets and drift rates. (2) Lease tests: acquire lease under various skew conditions (0ms, 10ms, 100ms, 1s), verify correct expiry. (3) Budget tests: propagate budget through actors with different clock offsets, verify deadline correctness. (4) Heartbeat tests: verify heartbeat detection with skewed clocks. (5) Property tests (proptest): for any bounded clock skew (< 1s), all protocols eventually complete. (6) Integration: run full saga under clock skew via FrankenLab scenario, verify identical outcome to non-skewed execution.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:59.965023589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:34.683055179Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:22.798011739Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clock-skew","fault-injection","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1409,"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented clock skew fault injection. SkewClock wraps TimeSource with static offset, drift, one-time jump, and jitter. Integrates with TimerDriver. 18 unit + 15 integration tests, clippy clean, fmt clean. Commit 59eec7f.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2ktrc.5","title":"[TESTING] Loom-based concurrency tests for scheduler queues","description":"Implement Loom-based concurrency tests for Asupersync's scheduler queues and lock-free data structures. Loom exhaustively explores all possible thread interleavings to find concurrency bugs that are nearly impossible to catch with random testing.

BACKGROUND (Tier C Formal Assurance, Graveyard section 0.14): Loom provides model-checked concurrency testing by controlling thread scheduling and exploring all valid interleavings. Unlike stress tests that probabilistically find bugs, loom guarantees that if a bug exists within the exploration bound, it will be found. This corresponds to Tier C (Deductive Verification) on the formal assurance ladder because it provides exhaustive coverage within bounds.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Add loom as a dev-dependency behind a cfg(loom) feature flag. (2) Replace std::sync::atomic with loom::sync::atomic in testable code using conditional imports: #[cfg(loom)] use loom::sync::atomic::AtomicUsize; #[cfg(not(loom))] use std::sync::atomic::AtomicUsize. (3) Create a separate test binary: tests/loom_tests.rs that only runs under cargo test --features loom. (4) Use loom::model(|| { ... }) to wrap each test case. (5) For work-stealing deque: test push/pop from owner thread, steal from multiple thief threads, verify no lost items and no duplicates. (6) For MPSC channel: test concurrent sends from N producers, single consumer recv, verify total ordering within each producer and no message loss. (7) For task state machine: test concurrent wake/cancel/complete transitions, verify exactly one terminal state is reached. (8) For budget counter: test concurrent decrements from N threads, verify counter never goes below zero and final value equals initial minus total decrements.

COVERAGE TARGETS: (1) Work-stealing deque: all push/pop/steal interleavings with 2 threads, 3 operations each. (2) MPSC channel: all send/recv interleavings with 3 senders, 1 receiver, 2 messages each. (3) Task state machine: all wake/cancel/complete interleavings with 2 threads. (4) Budget counter: all decrement/check interleavings with 3 threads, 2 decrements each.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) All loom tests pass with max_preemptions=2 (CI fast mode). (2) All loom tests pass with max_preemptions=4 (nightly thorough mode). (3) No data races detected by loom (loom catches these automatically via its memory model). (4) No deadlocks (all model executions terminate within bounded steps). (5) Correct final state in all explored interleavings. (6) Loom tests complete in < 5 minutes for CI, < 30 minutes for nightly.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each loom test logs: test_name, max_preemptions, total_interleavings_explored, execution_time_ms, pass_fail. On failure: log the specific interleaving that caused the failure (loom provides a trace of scheduling decisions). Summary at test suite completion: total_tests, total_interleavings, unique_bugs_found. All output in structured JSON for CI parsing.

TESTING APPROACH (meta): (1) Start with the simplest data structure (budget counter) to validate the loom test infrastructure. (2) Progressively add more complex structures. (3) Use loom::thread::yield_now() at critical points to increase exploration coverage. (4) Compare loom results against tokio-test stress tests to validate that loom catches bugs stress tests miss.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:09.545514942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:08:56.160289555Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","loom","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:15Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ktrc.6","title":"[TESTING] E2E test: deterministic replay parity across 1000 random schedules","description":"End-to-end test that validates Asupersync's deterministic replay guarantee across 1000 random schedules using the Lab runtime.

BACKGROUND: Deterministic replay is Asupersync's core value proposition -- the ability to record a concurrent execution and replay it identically on any machine. This test validates that promise at scale. The FrankenLab deterministic scheduler (bd-1hu19.2) controls all nondeterministic choices (thread scheduling, timer resolution, I/O ordering) and records them to a trace file. Replay uses the same trace to make identical choices.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Use FrankenLab's deterministic scheduler with random seed generation: let seed = rand::thread_rng().gen::<u64>(); (2) Test program: a realistic workload with multiple concurrent tasks, channels, obligations, cancellation, and budget propagation. At minimum: 10 tasks, 5 channels, 3 obligation types, 1 cancellation event, budget propagation through 3 levels. (3) For each of 1000 iterations: (a) Generate random seed, (b) Run workload under Lab scheduler with recording, (c) Capture output hash (SHA-256 of all observable side effects: task return values, channel message order, obligation lifecycle events), (d) Replay from recorded trace, (e) Capture replay output hash, (f) Assert record_hash == replay_hash. (4) Use rayon::par_iter for parallel execution of the 1000 iterations. (5) Store all seeds and hashes in a results CSV for analysis.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) 100% replay determinism: all 1000 iterations produce identical record/replay hashes. (2) Test completes in < 10 minutes on CI (parallelized). (3) Any replay divergence produces a detailed diff showing the first diverging event with full context. (4) Test is itself deterministic: running the test with the same meta-seed produces the same 1000 sub-seeds. (5) Cross-platform: hashes are identical on x86_64 and aarch64 (floating-point determinism verified).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Per-iteration structured JSON log: iteration_index, seed, record_hash, replay_hash, match (bool), record_time_ms, replay_time_ms, event_count. Summary log at completion: total_iterations, match_count, mismatch_count, determinism_rate (target 1.0), total_wall_time_ms, avg_replay_overhead_percent. On divergence: full event-level diff with timestamps, task IDs, and event types for both record and replay traces. All logs emitted to both stdout (CI visible) and JSONL file for offline analysis.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Smoke test: single iteration with known seed, verify manually computed expected hash. (2) Regression: if any iteration ever fails, add its seed to a pinned regression set that runs on every CI build. (3) Statistical: track determinism rate over time in a dashboard -- any rate below 1.0 is a P0 bug.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:16.147531077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:31:04.513934942Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic-replay","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ktrc.7","title":"[TESTING] E2E test: no obligation leaks under 10M random schedules","description":"End-to-end stress test that runs 10 million random schedules with the Lab runtime and verifies zero obligation leaks. For each schedule: (1) Spawn a random DAG of tasks with random obligations (SendPermit, Lease, Reserve), (2) Apply random faults (cancel, timeout, panic), (3) After quiescence, verify all obligations have been released (obligation counter == 0), (4) Log any leaks with full schedule trace for reproduction. Configuration: Use rayon for parallel execution across cores. Target: complete 10M schedules in <30 minutes on CI. Statistics: Track leak rate with exact binomial confidence interval — target is 0 leaks with 95% CI upper bound <1e-7. This is the ultimate obligation safety validation — if we can survive 10M random schedules without a leak, the system is robust.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:25.150268482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:26.303864660Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","obligation-leaks","stress","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2lqyk","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Macaroons for Decentralized Capability Attenuation","description":"Asupersync's Cx capability context is a natural fit for Macaroons (§11.8) — bearer tokens with decentralized attenuation. Currently, Cx capabilities are checked at runtime but cannot be attenuated (restricted) without creating new Cx instances. Macaroons allow any holder to add caveats (restrictions) to a capability token without contacting the issuer. This enables: (1) Delegation with restriction — pass a capability to a child task with reduced authority, (2) Time-limited capabilities — add expiry caveats, (3) Context-dependent capabilities — add caveats that check runtime conditions. Implementation: Extend Cx with HMAC-based macaroon chains. Each caveat is a predicate checked at capability use time. Key papers: Birgisson et al. 2014 'Macaroons: Cookies with Contextual Caveats'. Acceptance criteria: (1) Cx capabilities support caveat chains, (2) Any task can attenuate (but not escalate) capabilities, (3) Caveat verification is O(n) in number of caveats, (4) Evidence ledger records capability checks and caveat evaluations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:31.681182496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:15.460912285Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","capabilities","macaroons"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lqyk","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2lqyk.1","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Design Macaroon-based Cx capability token format","description":"Design the token format for Macaroon-based Cx capabilities. A macaroon consists of: (1) An identifier (capability name + scope), (2) A location hint (issuing subsystem), (3) A root key known only to the issuer, (4) An HMAC chain where each caveat adds a new HMAC layer. The format must support: (a) Binary serialization for zero-copy passing between tasks, (b) Human-readable debug representation for EvidenceLedger logging, (c) Version field for forward compatibility. Must define the caveat predicate language -- a simple expression DSL for time bounds, scope restrictions, rate limits, and custom predicates. Deliverable: A design doc with token format specification, caveat predicate grammar, and serialization format. Reference: graveyard section 11.8 and Birgisson et al. 2014. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: INFO: macaroon token format finalized -- version={version}, max_caveat_size={bytes}, supported_predicates=[{predicate_types}]. DEBUG: token format example rendered at each detail level (binary hex, debug display, JSONL). WARN: design review found potential issue: {issue_description}. Metrics: (design deliverable -- no runtime metrics, but define the metrics contract that implementations must follow): macaroon_verification_latency_us (histogram), macaroon_caveat_count (histogram), macaroon_token_size_bytes (histogram).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:16:39.171923866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:31:14.336607816Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T22:30:49.590269772Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","design","macaroons"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lqyk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2lqyk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:51Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1411,"issue_id":"bd-2lqyk.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented Macaroon token format in src/cx/macaroon.rs. 6 predicate types (TimeBefore, TimeAfter, RegionScope, TaskScope, MaxUses, Custom), HMAC-chained caveat signatures, binary serialization, constant-time verification. 26 unit tests. Commit b709a2c.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:31:14Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2lqyk.2","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Implement HMAC-based caveat chain for Cx","description":"Implement the core HMAC-based caveat chain mechanism for Cx capabilities. This is the cryptographic backbone of the Macaroon system. Each macaroon starts with HMAC(root_key, identifier). Adding a caveat computes HMAC(previous_mac, caveat_predicate) -- chaining so caveats cannot be removed without invalidating the HMAC. Verification walks the chain, checking each predicate and verifying the HMAC. Implementation: (1) HMAC-SHA256 via ring or hmac crate, (2) Caveat predicates as byte slices (parsed at verification time), (3) Third-party caveats use discharge macaroons, (4) Performance: <1us per caveat verification. Must handle: caveat ordering independence, nested macaroons, Cx lifetime management. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: macaroon::verify with token_id, caveat_count, trace_id. Span: macaroon::attenuate with token_id, new_caveat_type. DEBUG: HMAC chain step: caveat_index={idx}, caveat_type={type}, predicate_size={bytes}. INFO: macaroon verified successfully: token_id={id}, caveats_checked={n}, verification_time_us={lat}.  {"id":"bd-2m2","title":"[Transport] Implement Multipath Symbol Aggregator","description":"# Bead asupersync-2m2: Implement Multipath Symbol Aggregator

## Overview and Purpose

This bead implements the multipath symbol aggregation infrastructure for the asupersync transport layer. When symbols arrive from multiple network paths (for redundancy or performance), the aggregator:

1. **Path management**: Track and manage multiple transport paths with different characteristics
2. **Deduplication**: Identify and discard duplicate symbols received on different paths
3. **Reordering**: Buffer and reorder symbols to deliver them in correct sequence
4. **Bandwidth aggregation**: Combine bandwidth from multiple paths for higher throughput

The aggregator integrates with the routing layer (`asupersync-86i`) and feeds into the decoding pipeline. It's essential for RaptorQ's erasure coding to work correctly across unreliable multi-path networks.

## Core Types

```rust
//! Multipath symbol aggregation infrastructure.
//!
//! This module provides symbol aggregation from multiple transport paths:
//! - `TransportPath`: Represents a single transport path with characteristics
//! - `PathSet`: Manages multiple paths to a destination
//! - `SymbolDeduplicator`: Filters duplicate symbols
//! - `SymbolReorderer`: Buffers and reorders symbols
//! - `MultipathAggregator`: Main aggregation orchestrator

use crate::error::{Error, ErrorKind};
use crate::types::symbol::{ObjectId, Symbol, SymbolId};
use crate::types::{RegionId, Time};
use std::collections::{BTreeMap, HashMap, HashSet, VecDeque};
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, AtomicU64, Ordering};
use std::sync::{Arc, RwLock};

// ============================================================================
// Path Types
// ============================================================================

/// Unique identifier for a transport path.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct PathId(pub u64);

impl PathId {
    /// Creates a new path ID.
    #[must_use]
    pub const fn new(id: u64) -> Self {
        Self(id)
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Display for PathId {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "Path({})", self.0)
    }
}

/// State of a transport path.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum PathState {
    /// Path is active and healthy.
    Active,

    /// Path is experiencing issues but still usable.
    Degraded,

    /// Path is temporarily unavailable.
    Unavailable,

    /// Path has been permanently closed.
    Closed,
}

impl PathState {
    /// Returns true if the path can be used for receiving.
    #[must_use]
    pub const fn is_usable(&self) -> bool {
        matches!(self, Self::Active | Self::Degraded)
    }
}

/// Characteristics of a transport path.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct PathCharacteristics {
    /// Estimated latency in milliseconds.
    pub latency_ms: u32,

    /// Estimated bandwidth in bytes per second.
    pub bandwidth_bps: u64,

    /// Estimated packet loss rate (0.0 - 1.0).
    pub loss_rate: f64,

    /// Path jitter in milliseconds.
    pub jitter_ms: u32,

    /// Whether this is a primary path.
    pub is_primary: bool,

    /// Path priority (lower = higher priority).
    pub priority: u32,
}

impl Default for PathCharacteristics {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            latency_ms: 50,
            bandwidth_bps: 1_000_000, // 1 Mbps
            loss_rate: 0.01,          // 1%
            jitter_ms: 10,
            is_primary: false,
            priority: 100,
        }
    }
}

impl PathCharacteristics {
    /// Creates characteristics for a high-quality path.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn high_quality() -> Self {
        Self {
            latency_ms: 10,
            bandwidth_bps: 10_000_000, // 10 Mbps
            loss_rate: 0.001,          // 0.1%
            jitter_ms: 2,
            is_primary: true,
            priority: 10,
        }
    }

    /// Creates characteristics for a backup path.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn backup() -> Self {
        Self {
            latency_ms: 100,
            bandwidth_bps: 500_000, // 500 Kbps
            loss_rate: 0.05,        // 5%
            jitter_ms: 30,
            is_primary: false,
            priority: 200,
        }
    }

    /// Calculates an overall quality score (higher = better).
    #[must_use]
    pub fn quality_score(&self) -> f64 {
        let latency_score = 1000.0 / (self.latency_ms as f64 + 1.0);
        let bandwidth_score = (self.bandwidth_bps as f64).log10();
        let loss_score = 1.0 - self.loss_rate;
        let jitter_score = 100.0 / (self.jitter_ms as f64 + 1.0);

        // Weighted combination
        latency_score * 0.3 + bandwidth_score * 0.3 + loss_score * 0.3 + jitter_score * 0.1
    }
}

/// A transport path for symbol transmission.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct TransportPath {
    /// Unique identifier.
    pub id: PathId,

    /// Human-readable name.
    pub name: String,

    /// Current state.
    pub state: PathState,

    /// Path characteristics.
    pub characteristics: PathCharacteristics,

    /// Remote endpoint address.
    pub remote_address: String,

    /// Symbols received on this path.
    pub symbols_received: AtomicU64,

    /// Symbols lost/dropped on this path.
    pub symbols_lost: AtomicU64,

    /// Duplicate symbols received on this path.
    pub duplicates_received: AtomicU64,

    /// Last activity time.
    pub last_activity: RwLock<Time>,

    /// Creation time.
    pub created_at: Time,
}

impl TransportPath {
    /// Creates a new transport path.
    pub fn new(id: PathId, name: impl Into<String>, remote: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            id,
            name: name.into(),
            state: PathState::Active,
            characteristics: PathCharacteristics::default(),
            remote_address: remote.into(),
            symbols_received: AtomicU64::new(0),
            symbols_lost: AtomicU64::new(0),
            duplicates_received: AtomicU64::new(0),
            last_activity: RwLock::new(Time::ZERO),
            created_at: Time::ZERO,
        }
    }

    /// Sets path characteristics.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn with_characteristics(mut self, chars: PathCharacteristics) -> Self {
        self.characteristics = chars;
        self
    }

    /// Records symbol receipt.
    pub fn record_receipt(&self, now: Time) {
        self.symbols_received.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        *self.last_activity.write().expect("lock poisoned") = now;
    }

    /// Records a duplicate.
    pub fn record_duplicate(&self) {
        self.duplicates_received.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    }

    /// Records a loss.
    pub fn record_loss(&self) {
        self.symbols_lost.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
    }

    /// Returns the effective loss rate.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn effective_loss_rate(&self) -> f64 {
        let received = self.symbols_received.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
        let lost = self.symbols_lost.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
        let total = received + lost;
        if total == 0 {
            0.0
        } else {
            lost as f64 / total as f64
        }
    }

    /// Returns the duplicate rate.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn duplicate_rate(&self) -> f64 {
        let received = self.symbols_received.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
        let duplicates = self.duplicates_received.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
        if received == 0 {
            0.0
        } else {
            duplicates as f64 / received as f64
        }
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// Path Set
// ============================================================================

/// Policy for path selection.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum PathSelectionPolicy {
    /// Use all available paths.
    UseAll,

    /// Use only primary paths.
    PrimaryOnly,

    /// Use paths with best quality score.
    BestQuality { count: usize },

    /// Use paths by priority.
    ByPriority { count: usize },

    /// Round-robin across paths.
    RoundRobin,
}

impl Default for PathSelectionPolicy {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self::UseAll
    }
}

/// Manages a set of paths to a destination.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct PathSet {
    /// All registered paths.
    paths: RwLock<HashMap<PathId, Arc<TransportPath>>>,

    /// Selection policy.
    policy: PathSelectionPolicy,

    /// Round-robin counter.
    rr_counter: AtomicU64,

    /// Next path ID.
    next_id: AtomicU64,
}

impl PathSet {
    /// Creates a new path set.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn new(policy: PathSelectionPolicy) -> Self {
        Self {
            paths: RwLock::new(HashMap::new()),
            policy,
            rr_counter: AtomicU64::new(0),
            next_id: AtomicU64::new(0),
        }
    }

    /// Registers a new path.
    pub fn register(&self, path: TransportPath) -> PathId {
        let id = path.id;
        let arc = Arc::new(path);
        self.paths.write().expect("lock poisoned").insert(id, arc);
        id
    }

    /// Creates and registers a new path.
    pub fn create_path(
        &self,
        name: impl Into<String>,
        remote: impl Into<String>,
        chars: PathCharacteristics,
    ) -> PathId {
        let id = PathId(self.next_id.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst));
        let path = TransportPath::new(id, name, remote).with_characteristics(chars);
        self.register(path)
    }

    /// Gets a path by ID.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn get(&self, id: PathId) -> Option<Arc<TransportPath>> {
        self.paths.read().expect("lock poisoned").get(&id).cloned()
    }

    /// Removes a path.
    pub fn remove(&self, id: PathId) -> Option<Arc<TransportPath>> {
        self.paths.write().expect("lock poisoned").remove(&id)
    }

    /// Returns all usable paths based on the selection policy.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn select_paths(&self) -> Vec<Arc<TransportPath>> {
        let paths = self.paths.read().expect("lock poisoned");
        let usable: Vec<_> = paths
            .values()
            .filter(|p| p.state.is_usable())
            .cloned()
            .collect();

        match self.policy {
            PathSelectionPolicy::UseAll => usable,

            PathSelectionPolicy::PrimaryOnly => usable
                .into_iter()
                .filter(|p| p.characteristics.is_primary)
                .collect(),

            PathSelectionPolicy::BestQuality { count } => {
                let mut sorted = usable;
                sorted.sort_by(|a, b| {
                    b.characteristics
                        .quality_score()
                        .partial_cmp(&a.characteristics.quality_score())
                        .unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)
                });
                sorted.into_iter().take(count).collect()
            }

            PathSelectionPolicy::ByPriority { count } => {
                let mut sorted = usable;
                sorted.sort_by_key(|p| p.characteristics.priority);
                sorted.into_iter().take(count).collect()
            }

            PathSelectionPolicy::RoundRobin => {
                if usable.is_empty() {
                    return vec![];
                }
                let idx = self.rr_counter.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed) as usize;
                vec![usable[idx % usable.len()].clone()]
            }
        }
    }

    /// Updates path state.
    pub fn set_state(&self, id: PathId, state: PathState) -> bool {
        if let Some(path) = self.paths.read().expect("lock poisoned").get(&id) {
            // Note: In real impl, path.state would need interior mutability
            true
        } else {
            false
        }
    }

    /// Returns the number of paths.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn count(&self) -> usize {
        self.paths.read().expect("lock poisoned").len()
    }

    /// Returns the number of usable paths.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn usable_count(&self) -> usize {
        self.paths
            .read()
            .expect("lock poisoned")
            .values()
            .filter(|p| p.state.is_usable())
            .count()
    }

    /// Returns aggregate statistics.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn stats(&self) -> PathSetStats {
        let paths = self.paths.read().expect("lock poisoned");

        let mut total_received = 0u64;
        let mut total_lost = 0u64;
        let mut total_duplicates = 0u64;
        let mut total_bandwidth = 0u64;

        for path in paths.values() {
            total_received += path.symbols_received.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
            total_lost += path.symbols_lost.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
            total_duplicates += path.duplicates_received.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
            if path.state.is_usable() {
                total_bandwidth += path.characteristics.bandwidth_bps;
            }
        }

        PathSetStats {
            path_count: paths.len(),
            usable_count: paths.values().filter(|p| p.state.is_usable()).count(),
            total_received,
            total_lost,
            total_duplicates,
            aggregate_bandwidth_bps: total_bandwidth,
        }
    }
}

/// Configuration for deduplication.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct DeduplicatorConfig {
    /// Maximum symbols to track per object.
    pub max_symbols_per_object: usize,

    /// Maximum objects to track.
    pub max_objects: usize,

    /// TTL for deduplication entries.
    pub entry_ttl: Time,

    /// Whether to track receive path.
    pub track_path: bool,
}

impl Default for DeduplicatorConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            max_symbols_per_object: 10_000,
            max_objects: 1_000,
            entry_ttl: Time::from_secs(300),
            track_path: true,
        }
    }
}

/// Tracks seen symbols for deduplication.
#[derive(Debug)]
struct ObjectDeduplicationState {
    /// Symbols seen for this object.
    seen: HashSet<SymbolId>,

    /// When each symbol was first seen.
    first_seen: HashMap<SymbolId, Time>,

    /// Which path each symbol arrived on first.
    first_path: HashMap<SymbolId, PathId>,

    /// When this state was created.
    created_at: Time,

    /// Last activity time.
    last_activity: Time,
}

impl ObjectDeduplicationState {
    fn new(created_at: Time) -> Self {
        Self {
            seen: HashSet::new(),
            first_seen: HashMap::new(),
            first_path: HashMap::new(),
            created_at,
            last_activity: created_at,
        }
    }
}

/// Filters duplicate symbols across multiple paths.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct SymbolDeduplicator {
    /// Per-object deduplication state.
    objects: RwLock<HashMap<ObjectId, ObjectDeduplicationState>>,

    /// Configuration.
    config: DeduplicatorConfig,

    /// Total duplicates detected.
    duplicates_detected: AtomicU64,

    /// Total unique symbols processed.
    unique_symbols: AtomicU64,
}

impl SymbolDeduplicator {
    /// Creates a new deduplicator.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn new(config: DeduplicatorConfig) -> Self {
        Self {
            objects: RwLock::new(HashMap::new()),
            config,
            duplicates_detected: AtomicU64::new(0),
            unique_symbols: AtomicU64::new(0),
        }
    }

    /// Checks if a symbol is a duplicate.
    ///
    /// Returns `true` if the symbol is new (not a duplicate).
    /// Returns `false` if the symbol has been seen before.
    pub fn check_and_record(&self, symbol: &Symbol, path: PathId, now: Time) -> bool {
        let object_id = symbol.object_id();
        let symbol_id = symbol.id();

        let mut objects = self.objects.write().expect("lock poisoned");

        // Get or create object state
        let state = objects.entry(object_id).or_insert_with(|| {
            ObjectDeduplicationState::new(now)
        });

        // Check if already seen
        if state.seen.contains(&symbol_id) {
            self.duplicates_detected.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
            return false;
        }

        // Record new symbol
        state.seen.insert(symbol_id);
        state.first_seen.insert(symbol_id, now);
        if self.config.track_path {
            state.first_path.insert(symbol_id, path);
        }
        state.last_activity = now;

        self.unique_symbols.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        true
    }

    /// Returns the path that first delivered a symbol.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn first_path(&self, object_id: ObjectId, symbol_id: SymbolId) -> Option<PathId> {
        let objects = self.objects.read().expect("lock poisoned");
        objects
            .get(&object_id)
            .and_then(|state| state.first_path.get(&symbol_id).copied())
    }

    /// Prunes old entries.
    pub fn prune(&self, now: Time) -> usize {
        let mut objects = self.objects.write().expect("lock poisoned");
        let ttl_nanos = self.config.entry_ttl.as_nanos();

        let mut pruned = 0;
        objects.retain(|_, state| {
            let age = now.as_nanos().saturating_sub(state.last_activity.as_nanos());
            let keep = age < ttl_nanos;
            if !keep {
                pruned += 1;
            }
            keep
        });

        pruned
    }

    /// Returns statistics.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn stats(&self) -> DeduplicatorStats {
        let objects = self.objects.read().expect("lock poisoned");
        let total_tracked: usize = objects.values().map(|s| s.seen.len()).sum();

        DeduplicatorStats {
            objects_tracked: objects.len(),
            symbols_tracked: total_tracked,
            duplicates_detected: self.duplicates_detected.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
            unique_symbols: self.unique_symbols.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
        }
    }

    /// Clears all state for an object (e.g., after decoding completes).
    pub fn clear_object(&self, object_id: ObjectId) {
        self.objects.write().expect("lock poisoned").remove(&object_id);
    }
}

}

impl Default for ReordererConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            max_buffer_per_object: 1_000,

        }
    }
}

/// Buffered symbol waiting for delivery.
#[derive(Debug)]
struct BufferedSymbol {
    /// The symbol.
    symbol: Symbol,
    /// When it was received.
    received_at: Time,
    /// Path it was received on.
    path: PathId,
}

/// Per-object reordering state.
#[derive(Debug)]
struct ObjectReorderState {
    /// Next expected sequence number.
    next_expected: u32,

    /// Buffered out-of-order symbols (keyed by sequence).
    buffer: BTreeMap<u32, BufferedSymbol>,

    /// Last delivery time.
    last_delivery: Time,
}

impl ObjectReorderState {
    fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            next_expected: 0,
            buffer: BTreeMap::new(),
            last_delivery: Time::ZERO,
        }
    }
}

/// Buffers and reorders symbols to deliver in sequence.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct SymbolReorderer {
    /// Per-object reordering state.
    objects: RwLock<HashMap<ObjectId, ObjectReorderState>>,

    /// Configuration.
    config: ReordererConfig,

    /// Symbols delivered in order.
    in_order_deliveries: AtomicU64,

    /// Symbols delivered out of order (after buffering).
    reordered_deliveries: AtomicU64,

    /// Symbols that timed out waiting.
    timeout_deliveries: AtomicU64,
}

impl SymbolReorderer {
    /// Creates a new reorderer.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn new(config: ReordererConfig) -> Self {
        Self {
            objects: RwLock::new(HashMap::new()),
            config,
            in_order_deliveries: AtomicU64::new(0),
            reordered_deliveries: AtomicU64::new(0),
            timeout_deliveries: AtomicU64::new(0),
        }
    }

    /// Processes an incoming symbol.
    ///
    /// Returns symbols ready for delivery (may be empty, one, or multiple).
    pub fn process(&self, symbol: Symbol, path: PathId, now: Time) -> Vec<Symbol> {
        if self.config.immediate_delivery {
            return vec![symbol];
        }

        let object_id = symbol.object_id();
        let seq = symbol.symbol_index();

        let mut objects = self.objects.write().expect("lock poisoned");
        let state = objects.entry(object_id).or_insert_with(ObjectReorderState::new);

        let mut ready = Vec::new();

        // Check if this is the expected symbol
        if seq == state.next_expected {
            // Deliver immediately
            ready.push(symbol);
            state.next_expected += 1;
            state.last_delivery = now;
            self.in_order_deliveries.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);

            // Check buffer for consecutive symbols
            while let Some(buffered) = state.buffer.remove(&state.next_expected) {
                ready.push(buffered.symbol);
                state.next_expected += 1;
                self.reordered_deliveries.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
            }
        } else if seq > state.next_expected {
            // Out of order - buffer it
            let gap = seq - state.next_expected;
            if gap <= self.config.max_sequence_gap
                && state.buffer.len() < self.config.max_buffer_per_object
            {
                state.buffer.insert(
                    seq,
                    BufferedSymbol {
                        symbol,
                        received_at: now,
                        path,
                    },
                );
            }
            // else: gap too large or buffer full, drop the symbol
        }
        // else: seq < next_expected - this is a late duplicate, ignore

        ready
    }

    /// Flushes timed-out symbols.
    ///
    /// Returns symbols that have waited too long.
    pub fn flush_timeouts(&self, now: Time) -> Vec<Symbol> {
        let mut objects = self.objects.write().expect("lock poisoned");
        let max_wait_nanos = self.config.max_wait_time.as_nanos();
        let mut flushed = Vec::new();

        for state in objects.values_mut() {
            let mut to_remove = Vec::new();

            for (&seq, buffered) in &state.buffer {
                let wait_time = now.as_nanos().saturating_sub(buffered.received_at.as_nanos());
                if wait_time >= max_wait_nanos {
                    to_remove.push(seq);
                }
            }

            for seq in to_remove {
                if let Some(buffered) = state.buffer.remove(&seq) {
                    flushed.push(buffered.symbol);
                    self.timeout_deliveries.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
                }
            }

            // Advance next_expected if we flushed waiting symbols
            while state.buffer.get(&state.next_expected).is_none() {
                if state.buffer.is_empty()
                    || *state.buffer.keys().next().unwrap() > state.next_expected
                {
                    // Check if we need to skip ahead
                    if let Some(&first_key) = state.buffer.keys().next() {
                        if first_key > state.next_expected + self.config.max_sequence_gap {
                            state.next_expected = first_key;
                        }
                    }
                    break;
                }
                state.next_expected += 1;
            }
        }

        flushed
    }

    /// Returns statistics.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn stats(&self) -> ReordererStats {
        let objects = self.objects.read().expect("lock poisoned");
        let total_buffered: usize = objects.values().map(|s| s.buffer.len()).sum();

        ReordererStats {
            objects_tracked: objects.len(),
            symbols_buffered: total_buffered,
            in_order_deliveries: self.in_order_deliveries.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
            reordered_deliveries: self.reordered_deliveries.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
            timeout_deliveries: self.timeout_deliveries.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
        }
    }

    /// Clears state for an object.
    pub fn clear_object(&self, object_id: ObjectId) {
        self.objects.write().expect("lock poisoned").remove(&object_id);
    }
}

}

/// Result of processing a symbol.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct ProcessResult {
    /// Symbols ready for delivery to decoder.
    pub ready: Vec<Symbol>,

    /// Whether the symbol was a duplicate.
    pub was_duplicate: bool,

    /// Path the symbol arrived on.
    pub path: PathId,
}

/// The main multipath aggregator.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct MultipathAggregator {
    /// Path set.
    paths: Arc<PathSet>,

    /// Deduplicator.
    dedup: SymbolDeduplicator,

    /// Reorderer.
    reorderer: SymbolReorderer,

    /// Configuration.
    config: AggregatorConfig,

    /// Total symbols processed.
    total_processed: AtomicU64,

    /// Last flush time.
    last_flush: RwLock<Time>,
}

impl MultipathAggregator {
    /// Creates a new aggregator.
    pub fn new(config: AggregatorConfig) -> Self {
        let paths = Arc::new(PathSet::new(config.path_policy));

        Self {
            paths,
            dedup: SymbolDeduplicator::new(config.dedup.clone()),
            reorderer: SymbolReorderer::new(config.reorder.clone()),
            config,
            total_processed: AtomicU64::new(0),
            last_flush: RwLock::new(Time::ZERO),
        }
    }

    /// Returns the path set for configuration.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn paths(&self) -> &Arc<PathSet> {
        &self.paths
    }

    /// Processes an incoming symbol from a path.
    pub fn process(&self, symbol: Symbol, path: PathId, now: Time) -> ProcessResult {
        self.total_processed.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);

        // Record path activity
        if let Some(p) = self.paths.get(path) {
            p.record_receipt(now);
        }

        // Check for duplicates
        let is_unique = self.dedup.check_and_record(&symbol, path, now);

        if !is_unique {
            // Duplicate - record and discard
            if let Some(p) = self.paths.get(path) {
                p.record_duplicate();
            }
            return ProcessResult {
                ready: vec![],
                was_duplicate: true,
                path,
            };
        }

        // Process through reorderer if enabled
        let ready = if self.config.enable_reordering {
            self.reorderer.process(symbol, path, now)
        } else {
            vec![symbol]
        };

        ProcessResult {
            ready,
            was_duplicate: false,
            path,
        }
    }

    /// Flushes any timed-out symbols.
    pub fn flush(&self, now: Time) -> Vec<Symbol> {
        // Check flush interval
        {
            let last = *self.last_flush.read().expect("lock poisoned");
            let interval_nanos = self.config.flush_interval.as_nanos();
            if now.as_nanos().saturating_sub(last.as_nanos()) < interval_nanos {
                return vec![];
            }
            *self.last_flush.write().expect("lock poisoned") = now;
        }

        // Flush reorderer timeouts
        let mut flushed = self.reorderer.flush_timeouts(now);

        // Prune deduplicator
        self.dedup.prune(now);

        flushed
    }

    /// Notifies that an object has been fully decoded.
    ///
    /// Clears all state for the object.
    pub fn object_complete(&self, object_id: ObjectId) {
        self.dedup.clear_object(object_id);
        self.reorderer.clear_object(object_id);
    }

    /// Returns aggregate statistics.
    #[must_use]
    pub fn stats(&self) -> AggregatorStats {
        AggregatorStats {
            paths: self.paths.stats(),
            dedup: self.dedup.stats(),
            reorder: self.reorderer.stats(),
            total_processed: self.total_processed.load(Ordering::Relaxed),
        }
    }
}

/// Errors from aggregation.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum AggregationError {
    /// Path not found.
    PathNotFound { path: PathId },

    /// Path is unavailable.
    PathUnavailable { path: PathId },

    /// Buffer overflow.
    BufferOverflow { object_id: ObjectId },

    /// Invalid symbol sequence.
    InvalidSequence {
        object_id: ObjectId,
        expected: u32,
        received: u32,
    },
}

impl std::fmt::Display for AggregationError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            Self::PathNotFound { path } => write!(f, "path {} not found", path),
            Self::PathUnavailable { path } => write!(f, "path {} unavailable", path),
            Self::BufferOverflow { object_id } => {
                write!(f, "buffer overflow for object {:?}", object_id)
            }
            Self::InvalidSequence {
                object_id,
                expected,
                received,
            } => {
                write!(
                    f,
                    "invalid sequence for object {:?}: expected {}, got {}",
                    object_id, expected, received
                )
            }
        }
    }
}

impl std::error::Error for AggregationError {}

impl From<AggregationError> for Error {
    fn from(e: AggregationError) -> Self {
        Error::new(ErrorKind::StreamEnded).with_context(e.to_string())
    }
}
```

|------|-------------|

### Key Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|

| `PathSet::select_paths()` | Get usable paths per policy |
| `SymbolDeduplicator::check_and_record()` | Check if symbol is duplicate |
| `SymbolReorderer::process()` | Buffer or deliver symbol |

| `MultipathAggregator::flush()` | Flush timeouts |

## Integration Patterns

### Pattern 1: Setting Up Multipath Reception

```rust
fn setup_multipath() -> MultipathAggregator {
    let config = AggregatorConfig {
        path_policy: PathSelectionPolicy::UseAll,
        enable_reordering: true,
        dedup: DeduplicatorConfig {
            max_symbols_per_object: 50_000,
            ..Default::default()
        },
        reorder: ReordererConfig {
            max_wait_time: Time::from_millis(50),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        ..Default::default()
    };

    let aggregator = MultipathAggregator::new(config);

    // Register paths
    aggregator.paths().create_path(
        "primary",
        "10.0.0.1:8080",
        PathCharacteristics::high_quality(),
    );

    aggregator.paths().create_path(
        "backup",
        "10.0.0.2:8080",
        PathCharacteristics::backup(),
    );

    aggregator
}
```

### Pattern 2: Processing Incoming Symbols

```rust
async fn receive_loop(
    aggregator: &MultipathAggregator,
    decoder: &mut Decoder,
) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Error> {
    loop {
        let (symbol, path_id) = receive_from_network().await?;
        let now = get_current_time();

        let result = aggregator.process(symbol, path_id, now);

        if result.was_duplicate {
            log::debug!("Duplicate symbol from path {}", result.path);
            continue;
        }

        for ready_symbol in result.ready {
            decoder.add_symbol(ready_symbol);

            if decoder.can_decode() {
                let data = decoder.decode()?;
                aggregator.object_complete(ready_symbol.object_id());
                return Ok(data);
            }
        }

        // Periodic flush
        for flushed in aggregator.flush(now) {
            decoder.add_symbol(flushed);
        }
    }
}
```

### Pattern 3: Dynamic Path Management

```rust
fn monitor_paths(aggregator: &MultipathAggregator) {
    let stats = aggregator.paths().stats();

    for path in aggregator.paths().select_paths() {
        let loss_rate = path.effective_loss_rate();

        if loss_rate > 0.1 {
            // High loss - mark degraded
            log::warn!("Path {} has high loss rate: {:.2}%", path.id, loss_rate * 100.0);
            aggregator.paths().set_state(path.id, PathState::Degraded);
        }
    }

    log::info!(
        "Path stats: {} usable / {} total, {:.2} Mbps aggregate",
        stats.usable_count,
        stats.path_count,
        stats.aggregate_bandwidth_bps as f64 / 1_000_000.0
    );
}
```

### Pattern 4: Bandwidth Aggregation Estimation

```rust
fn estimate_aggregate_throughput(aggregator: &MultipathAggregator) -> u64 {
    let paths = aggregator.paths().select_paths();

    let total_bps: u64 = paths
        .iter()
        .map(|p| {
            // Adjust for loss
            let effective = p.characteristics.bandwidth_bps as f64 * (1.0 - p.effective_loss_rate());
            effective as u64
        })
        .sum();

    // Account for deduplication overhead
    let dedup_stats = aggregator.stats().dedup;
    let dedup_overhead = if dedup_stats.unique_symbols > 0 {
        dedup_stats.duplicates_detected as f64 / dedup_stats.unique_symbols as f64
    } else {
        0.0
    };

    let effective_bps = total_bps as f64 / (1.0 + dedup_overhead);
    effective_bps as u64
}
```

## Logging Strategy

```rust
use crate::observability::{LogEntry, LogLevel};

impl TransportPath {
    fn log_state_change(&self, old: PathState, new: PathState) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::info("Path state changed")
            .with_field("path_id", &format!("{}", self.id))
            .with_field("name", &self.name)
            .with_field("from", &format!("{:?}", old))
            .with_field("to", &format!("{:?}", new))
    }
}

impl SymbolDeduplicator {
    fn log_duplicate(&self, symbol: &Symbol, path: PathId) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::debug("Duplicate symbol detected")
            .with_field("object_id", &format!("{:?}", symbol.object_id()))
            .with_field("symbol_id", &format!("{:?}", symbol.id()))
            .with_field("path", &format!("{}", path))
    }
}

impl SymbolReorderer {
    fn log_timeout(&self, symbol: &Symbol) -> LogEntry {
        LogEntry::debug("Symbol delivered after timeout")
            .with_field("object_id", &format!("{:?}", symbol.object_id()))
            .with_field("sequence", &format!("{}", symbol.symbol_index()))
    }
}

impl MultipathAggregator {
    fn log_stats(&self) -> LogEntry {
        let stats = self.stats();
        LogEntry::info("Aggregator statistics")
            .with_field("paths", &format!("{}", stats.paths.path_count))
            .with_field("processed", &format!("{}", stats.total_processed))
            .with_field("duplicates", &format!("{}", stats.dedup.duplicates_detected))
            .with_field("reordered", &format!("{}", stats.reorder.reordered_deliveries))
    }
}
```
